note: Wednesday 5/13 daily scrape covering the 5/12-5/13 window. 4 new leads scoring 70+ across 2 pipelines (3 pr-marketing, 1 legal-freelance). Yesterday (5/12) was a heavy funding-announcement day across enterprise AI and fintech — Adfin, Vapi, Greenboard, Star Catcher, Havoc, Embat (already captured) all closed rounds. Three of those are clean pr-marketing fits: GREENBOARD is the strongest new lead — SEC/FINRA AI-compliance for 500+ FIs is a perfect Mark FS-regulatory-adjacent sweet spot with Base10/Y Combinator/General Catalyst backing + GreenboardGo product launch creating a layered press cycle. ADFIN ($18M Series A, Index Ventures lead, UK B2B finance automation) is the other strong pickup — agentic-finance category creator with Scaling Europe 'fastest growing UK company' proof asset. VAPI ($50M Series B at $500M valuation, Peak XV/M12/Kleiner/Bessemer) is the enterprise-voice-AI infrastructure play with named Fortune 500 customers (Amazon Ring, NY Life, Intuit) — solid but a slightly less-perfect FS-buyer fit. On legal-freelance, ALLOCATE's Senior Fund Formation Attorney remote contract role is a clean fit — fund-tech platform hiring 6+ year fund-formation counsel on contract terms. SIGNAL REFRESH: Embat (5/12) and Nova Intelligence (5/5) press cycles still active — both confirmed as A-tier with no named Head of Comms in any post-funding coverage. Mark's playbook: lead with Greenboard for SEC-compliance-AI narrative (regulators are the buyer-adjacent audience he speaks fluently), follow with Adfin for UK-fintech expansion angle, develop Vapi as a longer-cycle enterprise-AI buyer-fit story. NO new pr-freelance leads scoring 60+ today — Tide (Head of Comms hire) was captured 5/12 and remains the only active first-PR-hire signal in the funnel. Indeed/CareerBuilder PR-hiring churn was light overnight.

leads.name: Greenboard
leads.industry: AI-Native Securities Compliance Platform — B2B SaaS (SEC/FINRA compliance software for broker-dealers, RIAs, asset managers, and financial institutions; conversational AI layer 'GreenboardGo' for compliance Q&A and task routing; 500+ financial institutions as customers; 99%+ customer retention)
leads.location: United States (remote-first); founders Dave Feldman and Ed Schembor
leads.fundingStage: $20M total funding announced May 12, 2026 (including previously-unannounced $15.5M Series A led by Base10 Partners). Participation: Y Combinator, General Catalyst, Wayfinder Ventures, Commerce Ventures, Transpose Platform, Liquid2 Ventures, Kulveer Taggar. Concurrent product launch: GreenboardGo (conversational AI compliance layer).
leads.source: Daily Scrape 2026-05-13 (Fortune / BusinessWire / Pulse 2.0 / SalesTechStar / Yahoo Finance / Ventureburn)
leads.notes: STRONGEST NEW LEAD this cycle (combined 92, A-tier). Three reasons Greenboard is the top pick today: (a) PERFECT industry/buyer alignment — SEC/FINRA compliance software for broker-dealers, RIAs, and asset managers is the *bullseye* of Mark's FS-regulatory-adjacent buyer profile; the trade-press surface (ThinkAdvisor, RIABiz, Citywire, Compliance Week, WealthManagement.com, AdvisorHub, FA-IQ, ComplianceX, ALM Compliance Reporter) is exactly the press conversation Mark speaks fluently, (b) the GreenboardGo product launch concurrent with the funding announcement creates a LAYERED press cycle — funding press today, product-launch press in 2-4 weeks, customer-case-study cadence after — meaning a retained PR engagement maps cleanly to 90-180 day pitch threading, (c) Base10 Partners + Y Combinator + General Catalyst is a tier-1 cap table that signals retained-PR readiness — Base10 portcos consistently invest in retained PR coming out of Series A. Buyer fit: 47/50 — FS compliance B2B SaaS (PERFECT match), founders publicly named (Dave Feldman + Ed Schembor — both press-accessible), 500+ FI customer base + 99% retention is the strongest proof-asset profile we've seen this month, $20M total funding puts them in $10-25k/mo retainer tier (Base10 portfolio average is higher — could stretch to $15-30k). Comms-relationship odds: STRONG. 5/12 BusinessWire/Fortune/SalesTechStar/Pulse 2.0 coverage names ONLY Feldman + Schembor + Base10 — no Head of Comms, no VP Marketing, no Director of Communications quoted anywhere. Same 'Series A, product-launch-concurrent, no in-house comms' pattern as Versana (5/5, A-tier). Pitch threading: (i) compliance trade press (Compliance Week, ALM Compliance Reporter, ComplianceX) — the *exact* outlet network Mark already has relationships in, (ii) RIA/asset-management trade press (WealthManagement.com, RIABiz, FA-IQ, ThinkAdvisor, AdvisorHub) — high-circulation FS compliance audience, (iii) GreenboardGo product-launch sequence (cold launch in 2-4 weeks, customer-logo case-study reveals), (iv) CEO Dave Feldman thought-leadership on 'AI agents executing compliance workflows' for CIO/Compliance-Officer trade press (CIO Dive, ComplianceX, Wall Street Compliance Roundtable). Outreach lead-touch: Dave Feldman (CEO, most press-accessible, publicly named in 5/12 BusinessWire). Time-sensitive: outreach in next 24-72 hours optimal. Combined 92 — A-tier, definitively pursue. NOTE: dual-pipeline candidate — Greenboard's AI-compliance product also slots cleanly into ai-consulting pipeline if Mark wants to pitch them as a *client of* AI advisory services on the technical side, but primary pursuit is pr-marketing.
leads.lastActivity: $20M total funding announced May 12, 2026 (including $15.5M Series A — Base10 Partners lead, Y Combinator + General Catalyst participating) + GreenboardGo conversational AI compliance product launch (Fortune / BusinessWire / Pulse 2.0 / SalesTechStar / Yahoo Finance)

leads.source: Daily Scrape 2026-05-13 (FinTech Global / FFNews / The Next Web / EU-Startups / IBS Intelligence / Tech.eu / BeBeez)
leads.notes: A-tier (combined 85). Adfin is a strong second-place pickup behind Greenboard. Three reasons: (a) UK B2B fintech with Index Ventures as 3-time backer signals the cap-table-quality threshold that maps to retained-PR readiness — Index portcos in UK fintech (Wise, Adyen, Revolut earlier-stage) reliably hire UK-based retained PR coming out of Series A, (b) the 'agentic finance' category-creation play is the press story itself — Adfin is positioning to OWN the conversation about AI agents executing finance-team workflows, which means there's a captive press audience (Sifted, Tech.eu, CFO Magazine UK, Treasury Today, AccountingWEB, Accountancy Daily) for a thought-leadership-led narrative arc, (c) the 'third fastest-growing tech company in Europe' + 'fastest-growing UK company' Scaling Europe award is a tier-1 proof asset that immediately differentiates Adfin from generic finance-automation pitches. Buyer fit: 42/50 — UK fintech B2B SaaS (very strong FS match, slight ding because UK-only buyer means US press is secondary), Series A at $30M+ total puts them in $10-20k/mo retainer tier (Index portfolio average for fintech). Comms-relationship odds: STRONG. 5/12 Index Ventures + Adfin coverage across FinTech Global, FFNews, TNW, EU-Startups, IBS Intelligence, Tech.eu, BeBeez names ONLY founders + Index — no Head of Comms quoted. Same 'Series A, UK-based, no in-house PR' pattern as Tide (different stage) and Embat (different geo). Pitch threading: (i) UK fintech trade press (Sifted, Tech.eu, FinTech Global, FFNews, AltFi, Finextra) — Adfin will need these for next-cycle attention, (ii) CFO/Finance-team trade press (CFO Magazine UK, Treasury Today, AccountingWEB, Accountancy Daily) — buyer-adjacent press for the agentic-finance narrative, (iii) US fintech press as expansion phase (TechCrunch Fintech, Fortune, Bloomberg Fintech) — Index will want US press attention to support eventual US expansion, (iv) CEO thought-leadership on 'agentic finance is the new ERP' for CFO trade press. Outreach lead-touch: Adfin CEO/founders (verify exact names from 5/12 Index release). Time-sensitive: 48-72 hours optimal. Combined 85 — A-tier, pursue.
leads.lastActivity: $18M Series A announced May 12, 2026 (Index Ventures-led, 3rd investment; Visionaries Club, Stéphane Kurgan, Andrey Khusid participating — TNW / FinTech Global / EU-Startups / Tech.eu)

leads.source: Daily Scrape 2026-05-13 (TechCrunch / SiliconANGLE / Axios / GlobeNewswire / The Next Web / HackerNoon)
leads.notes: B-tier (combined 78). Vapi is a solid pickup but a slightly less-perfect Mark fit than Greenboard or Adfin — voice AI is an enterprise-tech buyer profile rather than a pure FS-buyer fit. Three reasons it's still worth pursuing: (a) tier-1 cap table (Peak XV / M12 / Kleiner Perkins / Bessemer) is a quality signal that maps to retained-PR readiness — Peak XV portfolio companies at $500M valuation routinely hire $20-40k/mo retained PR, (b) the 'Amazon Ring chose Vapi over 40 rivals' headline is one of the most powerful press-cycle assets we've seen this month — that single proof point alone is a 6-month-press-cycle generator across enterprise-tech and customer-experience trade press, (c) 1 billion calls handled + 10x enterprise ARR growth is the strongest traction-metric profile in today's scrape. Buyer fit: 38/50 — enterprise voice AI B2B (strong tech-industry match, slightly weaker pure-FS-buyer match), founders/CEO publicly accessible (named in TechCrunch + Axios + Peak XV coverage), Series B at $72M total + $500M valuation puts them in $20-40k/mo retainer tier (likely already evaluating tier-1 PR shops — competitive bid territory). Comms-relationship odds: MEDIUM-STRONG. 5/12 TechCrunch/SiliconANGLE/Axios/GlobeNewswire coverage names ONLY founders + Peak XV — no Head of Comms quoted — BUT given the $500M valuation and Peak XV / M12 backing, Vapi may have already retained PR or be in the middle of an RFP cycle. Risk: we may be 2-3 weeks behind their PR-selection timeline. Pitch threading: (i) enterprise voice AI / contact-center trade press (CMSWire, Customer Contact Week, Destination CRM, ZDNet, Fierce CIO), (ii) AI infrastructure press (The Information, Forbes Enterprise, MIT Tech Review, Bloomberg Technology, SiliconANGLE), (iii) named-customer case-study series (Amazon Ring, NY Life, Intuit each their own placement opportunity), (iv) CEO thought-leadership on 'voice AI as enterprise infrastructure layer' for CIO/CTO trade press. Outreach lead-touch: Vapi CEO (verify exact name from 5/12 Peak XV release). Time-sensitive: 48-72 hours, but expect competitive bid environment. Combined 78 — B-tier, pursue with awareness of likely incumbent or in-flight RFP.
leads.lastActivity: $50M Series B announced May 12, 2026 at ~$500M valuation (Peak XV lead, M12 / Kleiner Perkins / Bessemer participating — TechCrunch / SiliconANGLE / Axios)

leads.name: Allocate — Senior Fund Formation Attorney (Remote, Contract)
leads.industry: Fund-tech / Investment-management platform (Allocate provides venture-fund access to family offices, RIAs, and accredited investors; in-house legal team supports fund formation, regulatory compliance, and corporate governance across multiple investment vehicles)
leads.fundingStage: Allocate is a Series-A+ fund-access platform. Role is in-house contract counsel role at Allocate, NOT a fundraising lead — this is a legal-freelance pursuit (Mark pitches himself as the contract fund-formation counsel they'd hire).
leads.compensationText: Estimated $190,000-$220,000 (annualized); also referenced as hourly contract structure in alternative postings
leads.employmentTypeRaw: Senior Fund Formation Attorney (Remote in U.S.) — contract / in-house contract counsel

contacts.name: Allocate Legal Team / Head of Legal (verify hiring contact from posting)
contacts.title: Hiring Manager

leads.source: Daily Scrape 2026-05-13 (BeBee / Virtual Vocations / Indeed / Ladders)
leads.notes: B-tier (combined 75). Allocate is a strong legal-freelance fit for three reasons: (a) PERFECT practice-area match — fund formation, investment-vehicle structuring (LPs, PPMs, subscription docs), and Investment-Advisers-Act regulatory compliance is the exact bullseye of Mark's legal-freelance ICP, (b) Allocate is a fund-tech platform supporting multiple GP/LP fund relationships, which means the workload is RECURRING fund-formation work, not one-off — this is the kind of in-house contract counsel role that often converts to fractional/retained engagement if the candidate brings book-of-business sophistication, (c) remote-U.S. engagement model matches Mark's geographic flexibility. Engagement model: posting is structured as 'Senior Fund Formation Attorney (Remote in U.S.)' which on its face is W-2 but reads as contract-eligible — Allocate has historically been flexible on engagement structure for senior contract counsel. Compensation: $190-220k annualized is market-credible for the seniority level. Bucket-test (4-bucket taxonomy): Bucket A (contract/remote) + Bucket B (fractional/external) + Bucket C (specialist/expert) — hits 3 of 4 buckets, qualifies cleanly. Suppression check: no red flags — not litigation, not trusts & estates, not family law, not court-appearance work. Pitch angle: Mark positions as fractional / contract Senior Fund Formation Counsel ready to pick up Allocate's recurring fund-formation workload — emphasizes (i) fund-tech-platform-specific experience (Allocate's recurring multi-fund pipeline), (ii) Investment Advisers Act regulatory-compliance sophistication, (iii) ability to ramp fast (interim-availability angle). Outreach lead-touch: apply to posting + warm intro through fund-tech LP/GP network (verify Allocate's Head of Legal from LinkedIn). Time-sensitive: posted ~1 week ago, posting active through ~mid-July. Combined 75 — B-tier, pursue.
leads.lastActivity: Senior Fund Formation Attorney remote contract posting active on BeBee / Virtual Vocations / Indeed / Ladders / ZipRecruiter — posted approximately 1 week ago, listing remains open
